mnemonics: MONO means ONE. One alphabet, one fixed substitution rule from start to finish.
keyPoints: A simple substitution cipher where each letter in the plaintext is mapped to a fixed ciphertext letter.
The key is the entire substitution alphabet (the mapping of all 26 letters).
It is vulnerable to frequency analysis because it preserves the frequency patterns of the original language.
commonMistakes: Confusing it with the Caesar cipher (which is a specific type of monoalphabetic cipher with a shifted alphabet).
Assuming it is secure because the ciphertext looks scrambled, without considering frequency analysis attacks.
